violations.claim: State machine: cancelOrder's name-contract promises it only cancels
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 39
; State machine: cancelOrder's name-contract promises it only cancels
; orders for which cancellation is a valid transition.
; VALID_TRANSITIONS["shipped"]   = []  → cancel is NOT valid from shipped
; VALID_TRANSITIONS["cancelled"] = []  → cancel is NOT valid from cancelled
; The function body contains zero guards on pre_state.

(declare-const pre_state Int)
(declare-const post_state Int)
; is_valid_cancel = 1 iff pre_state ∈ {0,1,2} per VALID_TRANSITIONS
(declare-const is_valid_cancel Int)

; Code transition: cancelOrder sets no guard — any OrderState value reaches line 41
(assert (>= pre_state 0))
(assert (<= pre_state 4))

; Code transition: line 41 unconditionally sets state = 4
(assert (= post_state 4))

; Transition table encodes VALID_TRANSITIONS
(assert (=> (= pre_state 0) (= is_valid_cancel 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 1) (= is_valid_cancel 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 2) (= is_valid_cancel 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 3) (= is_valid_cancel 0)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 4) (= is_valid_cancel 0)))

; Violation: name-contract broken — mutation proceeds on invalid pre_state
(assert (= is_valid_cancel 0))

(check-sat)

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 39
; State machine: cancelOrder's name-contract promises it only cancels
; orders for which cancellation is a valid transition.
; VALID_TRANSITIONS["shipped"]   = []  → cancel is NOT valid from shipped
; VALID_TRANSITIONS["cancelled"] = []  → cancel is NOT valid from cancelled
; The function body contains zero guards on pre_state.

(declare-const pre_state Int)
(declare-const post_state Int)
; is_valid_cancel = 1 iff pre_state ∈ {0,1,2} per VALID_TRANSITIONS
(declare-const is_valid_cancel Int)

; Code transition: cancelOrder sets no guard — any OrderState value reaches line 41
(assert (>= pre_state 0))
(assert (<= pre_state 4))

; Code transition: line 41 unconditionally sets state = 4
(assert (= post_state 4))

; Transition table encodes VALID_TRANSITIONS
(assert (=> (= pre_state 0) (= is_valid_cancel 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 1) (= is_valid_cancel 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 2) (= is_valid_cancel 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 3) (= is_valid_cancel 0)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 4) (= is_valid_cancel 0)))

; Violation: name-contract broken — mutation proceeds on invalid pre_state
(assert (= is_valid_cancel 0))

(check-sat)
